channel.title: LLMs on Azlan Raban
channel.description: Recent content in LLMs on Azlan Raban

item.title: Backward Propagation in Large Language Models
item.description:
Unboxing the Black Box

As software engineers, it is easy to treat modern Large Language Models (LLMs) as magical black boxes. We send an API request, and a perfectly reasoned response comes back. But under the hood, whether it’s a massive transformer model or a simple classifier, the core mechanism driving how these networks learn remains largely unchanged from a breakthrough paper published in Nature back in October 1986: “Learning representations by back-propagating errors.”
